programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to what is one of the most eagerly anticipated weekends of the football calendar, when the FA Cup third round takes place

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. Experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young offer support, while away from the action Charlie sees a selection of of rare toys

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, with a £400,000 budget to find their ideal home near the English Riviera. Away from the property hunt, Ginny capitalises on being in Devon at the same time as the County Show to find out what it takes to win Pig of the Year

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the FA Cup, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Newcastle United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, which takes place at Emirates Stadium, as the holders take on Steve Bruce's Magpies. The Gunners have struggled badly in the Premier League so far this season, and may see this competition as a welcome distraction. The sides last met in this tournament at the fourth-round stage in 2008, when an Emmanuel Adebayor double and a Nicky Butt own goal sealed a routine 3-0 victory for Arsene Wenger's team

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob wants more from Connie, but she is unsure if will she be able to make the time for their relationship with mounting pressures on the emergency department. Meanwhile, Faith confronts Lev about his secret, and new paramedic Leon arrives to meet the rest of the team and complete a Covid-19 training course

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Festive instalment of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of the Wild Card play-off games, as the post-season begins

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned, then follow the sale and talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings. After three months the team returns to review their progress

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian advise two teams scouring London's vibrant Portobello Road for items to take to auction. Presented by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is in Stirling exploring the city's connection to the world-famous King James Bible, and JB Gill meets a Scottish singer who found Jesus while battling addiction

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Crawley Town v Leeds United (Kick-off 1.30pm). Coverage of the third-round tie at the People's Pension Stadium, where the League Two hosts are aiming to cause an upset. Crawley's league form this season has been inconsistent, leaving them firmly in mid-table, but they've already been involved in one of the most remarkable matches in this season's FA Cup, winning 6-5 away to Torquay United after extra time. They shocked AFC Wimbledon in round two, and are aiming to make a name for themselves against the Premier League visitors

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Bradley Stoke, Bristol, to come to the horticultural aid of Laura and Andrew, who require a water-saving, wildlife-friendly space

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Marine v Tottenham Hotspur (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the third-round encounter at Rossett Park, where the non-league side welcome Jose Mourinho's Premier League high-flyers. If ever a match epitomised the magic of the cup, then this is surely it, with Marine being only the second side from the eighth tier of English football to reach the third round. They have already won seven fixtures to reach this stage, including a penalty shootout triumph over League Two Colchester United in round one. Spurs are expected to rest several of their key players following a hectic festive period, but will be wary of being on the wrong end of one of the greatest upsets in the competition's history should they take Marine too lightly

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: After the turmoil of 2020 and as a new year gets underway, the team looks at how people can plan to make the countryside a bigger part of their lives? Matt Baker wants to get fitter and tries his hand at cani-cross, Anita Rani straps on her walking boots, Margherita Taylor heads for the woods and Helen Skelton aims to buy local. Tom Heap discovers the joys of 'blue' therapy and Adam Henson gets creative with clay

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth - the Sun - and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles befriends homesick young French backpacker Dominique Renelleau, introducing himself as Alain Gautier. After dinner, Dominique falls sick so Charles takes him back to his Bangkok apartment, where he promises that he and Marie-Andrée will nurse him back to health. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Action from the latest third-round fixtures, with Marine v Tottenham Hotspur, Newport County v Brighton & Hove Albion and Chelsea v Morecambe among the highlights. All three of the Premier League sides would have expected their class to tell against lower-league opposition, but the magic of the cup always suggests that a shock isn't too far around the corner. Plus, action from Barnsley v Tranmere Rovers, Bristol City v Portsmouth, Cheltenham Town v Mansfield Town, Crawley Town v Leeds United and Manchester City v Birmingham City

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: Flying Blind (2012)
programme.desc: An aerospace engineer designing military drones begins a passionate affair with a younger Muslim student. The relationship leads to her being questioned by the security services, who claim he is involved in terrorism, forcing her to question how much she knows about her lover and where her loyalties lie. Drama, starring Helen McCrory and Najib Oudghiri

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the scammers selling non-existent motorhomes to unsuspecting customers, and the cyber criminals who hijack bookings for holiday rentals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down bungalow in Earley in Berkshire, a Victorian terrace with lots of period features in Norwich and a pair of semis in Burton-on-Trent with a fabulous river view are all sold under the hammer. All the developers have ambitious plans for their auction buys and hope to make some money, so Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell monitor whether their ideas are realised

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After a terrible accident turned their lives upside down, Stan and Andrea thought Australia was the answer. Six years on, did the dream become reality? Nikki Chapman finds out

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. Charlie Ross presents

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Dave Myers enjoys a taste of his native county when he and Si King demonstrate recipes using produce from Cumbria, including a supper dish made with Cumberland sausage

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £450,000, a married couple looking to uproot from Greater Manchester seek the help of Jules Hudson to find a new family home close to the coast in Dorset

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families prepare for the farmers' market in historic Stokesley - rare-breed pig farmers Nigel and Benjamin, and the Mellanby family with their harvested vegetables

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy items, including two teddy bears, an Islamic tile, a painting, and a chair brought in by a 12-year-old boy. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley is stunned when she learns the truth, prompting her to take matters into her own hands. Denise is left questioning who she should believe

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben finds out that all Janey's suitors are part-time teachers and Susan discovers that employing Nick is not for the faint-hearted. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay, Zoe Wanamaker, Daniela Denby-Ashe and Kris Marshall

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: McKinven's initial enquiries ruffle feathers, while a furious Julie tears into Emma for dragging up the past. When Skye threatens Emma with a knife, it is clear that someone is hiding something. Molly Windsor stars.

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: The actors spot an opportunity to sabotage the show and while David is deflated to discover how people feel about him, Michael is overjoyed to discover how they feel about him. Anna and Georgia consider their options Comedy in which David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Struggling to get any more information from Ashley, Rex turns to a forensic psychologist for answers. A date for trial is set, but Ashley makes a shocking announcement

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: A guide to strengthening the immune system during cold and flu season, examining some of the so-called 'miracle' products, superfoods and supplements that claim to help

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a 'plane den' for 11-year-old Philip, in the hope that a place where he can indulge his fascination with the RAF will help him cope with his digestive illness

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari makes mega-loaded fries. These crunchy potato wedges topped with spicy flavours are the perfect snack to give the family all the energy they need to outwit one another during their game of mega hide-and-seek

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari is creating his family's favourite snack - choccy oat bars. His yummy snack, filled with oats, syrup and chocolate, is the perfect treat

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey, Adam and Mwaksy meet some very special animals at the zoo

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From adders to anacondas, wildlife expert Steve Backshall answers questions about snakes

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From budgies to beetles, wildlife expert Steve Backshall answers questions about animals that live in and around people's backyards

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian and his team continue down the Colorado River in antique wooden boats as they re-create the pioneering 280-mile expedition through the Grand Canyon that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Along the way they carry out the same surveying tasks as the original party with period equipment, including taking photographs with a 19th-century 3D camera

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's situation improves after she is let back into the gang, but the Whiskers suffer a tragedy when they battle with the Commandos

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups find themselves alone during a storm when their uncle Youssarian abandons his babysitting duties. Meanwhile, the Lazulis try to expand their territory and move a little too close for the Whiskers' liking

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Cheetah Family & Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife film-maker Gordon Buchanan gets close to wild cheetah in the Kalahari, experiencing first-hand the very real dangers these beautiful big cats face. The programme follows two females, each with a litter of cubs, as they navigate a harsh environment, dangerous wildlife and dwindling prey

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers look across the Channel to see how the French have influenced British food over the centuries. They prepare a roast chicken with 40 cloves of garlic, and a delicate mille feuille, and examine UK products in traditionally French areas of gastronomy, from award-winning English wines to Scottish garlic

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer unravels the magic behind the combination of sugar and spice to reveal why this exotic marriage works so well in the kitchen

programme.title: Black Narcissus (1947)
programme.desc: British nuns establish a school and hospital on a remote mountainside in the Himalayas, where the nursing sisters face isolation as well as native mistrust. However, it is the presence of a male land agent that takes a final toll on their peace of mind. Oscar-winning drama from directorial team Powell and Pressburger. With Deborah Kerr, David Farrar, Kathleen Byron, Flora Robson and Jean Simmons

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Examining how animals, including monkeys, wolves, bears, foxes and birds, cope with mountain life in the Himalayas, and how the harsh terrain has given many creatures remarkable strength. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public gather at the Imperial War Museum Duxford in Cambridgeshire to present their antiques, and Paul Martin finds out more about the Hawker Hurricane, famed for its role in the Battle of Britain

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Eight celebrities embark on an experimental adventure to Jaipur in India, to test whether they can set up a more rewarding retirement than in the UK. Wayne Sleep, Miriam Margolyes, Rosemary Shrager, Bobby George, Jan Leeming, Patti Boulaye, Roy Walker and Sylvester McCoy take over a private mansion in the heart of the old city and have to work out how to set up home together. In the opening episode, the group are shocked by the realities of everyday life in India, including the hectic roads, poor toilet facilities and animals wandering the streets

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Solent, one the busiest stretches of water in the UK, reports come in of a missing kite surfer. The crew of Lymington lifeboat station start searching, but as the hours pass they begin to fear the worst. Twenty miles to the west, the Poole crew are called out to save a father and daughter from drowning as they are swept out to sea, and at Chiswick, one of the few RNLI stations with a crew on permanent standby 24/7, an unusual casualty in the Thames leaves the crew attempting to save not just one but nine lives

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anneka Rice and Liz Carr head out on a treasure-hunting quest in the Midlands, joined by expert assistants Philip Serrell and James Braxton, before going to auction in Greenwich. Liz also learns how criminal investigations were brought into the modern age, while Anneka heads to a local farm - much to the bewilderment of the farmer

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the classic comedy about the Home Guard, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier. Bank manager George Mainwaring responds to the call to set up a Local Defence Volunteers platoon in the town of Walmington-on-Sea on the south coast of England, assembling a motley crew of locals to protect Britain from Nazi invasion. First shown in 1968

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: Another chance to see this interview with the best-selling author, who died last month. Famous for writing espionage thrillers, le Carre started as a real-life spy working for MI6, and wrote the first of his hugely popular books while in the secret service. In conversation with Mark Lawson, le Carre discusses his work and his books, including discusses his life and work the novels Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy and The Spy Who Came in from the Cold

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carré's 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: The Big Short (2015)
programme.desc: Four investors each become aware of a flaw in the housing market that makes a financial crisis in the next few years inevitable. Each comes up with a plan to profit from the coming economic meltdown, but as their predictions come true, they are confronted with the devastating consequences for ordinary people. Fact-based drama, starring Christian Bale, Steve Carell, Ryan Gosling and Brad Pitt

programme.title: Apprentice (2016)
programme.desc: A soft-spoken corrections officer transferred to a high-security prison is taken under the wing of the facility's chief executioner. Drama, starring Firdaus Rahman, Wan Hanafi Su and Mastura Ahmad. In Malay and English

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Monty Don and his team of horticultural experts feature in this selection of highlights from the 2020 series

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In November and December, the horticulturist places delicate perennials under shelter, cuts back plants and sweeps up fallen leaves as the first frosts hit the garden. Snowdrops begin to appear as she puts tulip bulbs into the ground, and her thoughts turn to next spring

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers are caught raiding their rivals' burrow and forced to beat a hasty retreat - but there are dire consequences. Meanwhile, Flower learns one of her daughters has a secret. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Trouble brews among the meerkats as the impact of two simultaneous pregnancies makes its mark. Youssarian is left to babysit Flower's second litter of the year, while Mozart tries to assert her authority on the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: Cheetah Family & Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Wildlife film-maker Gordon Buchanan gets close to wild cheetah in the Kalahari, experiencing first-hand the very real dangers these beautiful big cats face. With the help of guides Richard Satekge and Julius Mkhize, this programme follows two female cheetah, each with a litter of cubs, as they navigate harsh environments, dangerous wildlife and dwindling prey

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Judd Trump v David Gilbert. The tournament gets under way at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes with the opening first-round match, as Gilbert takes on the world number one. Trump has been the leading player in the world over the last year and his consistency means that Gilbert will need to produce his very best and progress to the quarter-finals, where Kyren Wilson or Jack Lisowski awaits the winner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott are at Grittleton House in Wiltshire, where items include a valuable camera and a 19th-century tribal fighting club. Paul Martin visits a Victorian school that looks the same today as it did 100 years ago

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Armadale, at the northernmost tip of the Scottish mainland, Storm Ciara is wreaking havoc for hill farmer Joyce. Meanwhile, Father and daughter team Frank and Georgia Hunter are also battling the storm on their mixed livestock farm in Cumbria, and when the storm ruptures a section of the mains water pipe, their water supply is cut off. Elsewhere, Helen and Graham deal with a missing calf

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest World Cup meetings, featuring women's downhill from St Anton in Austria and men's slalom action from Adelboden in Switzerland

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually, while Cherry Healey discovers the science behind smelly feet and tests brands of socks to see how they cope with the problem. Ruth Goodman looks back at how socks became a fashion statement in the 1980s and a vital piece of military kit in the First World War

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A festive edition of the business ideas contest, with Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assessing the profit-making potential of ideas perfect for the Christmas season. An inventor introduces his innovative new form of Christmas tree stand, while other entrepreneurs pitch their ideas for a range of sweets and a puzzling family game. Finally, the creator of a personalised comic-book realises he must make a big personal decision if he is to secure funding. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs ends in Finland, where she discovers that climate change has had a serious impact on many traditional trades. In the south of the country, she explores the timber trade by meeting a forest cleaner and a baker. Venturing north, where temperatures are rising the most, she gets to grips with the tourist industry by training as an ice carver and an elf in Santa's village

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: On the second leg of her journey, Miriam is intrigued by the Australian concept of 'mateship' and how Australia is the only country in the world to enshrine this in its culture. Her first stop is a roadhouse south of Alice Springs, where she meets one of the few women truckers in Australia. She also encounters Aboriginal elders, who set up the Children's Ground to pass Arrernte traditions, songs, dances and stories on to the younger generation. Arriving in Darwin, Miriam encounters a group of transgender Tiwi islanders, who invite her to a drag night

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: On the final part of her journey, the actress visits Gulf of Carpentaria to see where the wealth of the 'Lucky Country' comes from. At the McArthur River Mine, she is taken aback by the enormity and scale of this operation. Miriam arrives in suburban Brisbane where she meets one of the wealthiest couples in Australia and visits their wildlife research centre, where they are working to save the koala from extinction. After eight weeks and 10,000kms, Miriam's final stop is Burleigh Heads on the Gold Coast, where she accompanies a family taking part in 'Nippers', a surf education program for young Australians

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Jack Lisowski. Highlights of the first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where a place in the quarter-final awaited the winner

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Action from two first-round matches on day one at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring Judd Trump v David Gilbert and Kyren Wilson v Jack Lisowski

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Part two of two. As the eclipse continues to create chaos, Sylar and Elle face the wrath of a vengeful Noah. Peter and Nathan help the Haitian battle his brother Baron Samedi, while Ando, Sam and Frack work to restore Hiro's memory with the help of the 9th Wonders comic book. Guest starring Seth Green (Family Guy)

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: As their battle against Arthur continues, Hiro and Claire travel 16 years back in time to when Kaito gave her to Noah. Peter and the Haitian team up to finish off Arthur for good, but Sylar slows their efforts. Meanwhile in the lab, Suresh has a breakthrough with the formula, which could change everything

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha buries his head in the sand when Beka is taken ill on New Year's Eve. Kian is on edge as Sarah Jane avoids his calls, and his stash is running out. He tries to convince Jac he is fine when she worries that their relationship is the problem. It is total chaos on AAU, and the F1s are daunted when Hanssen leaves them alone to face their first New Year's night shift

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After losing her father, Cheryl Castling was determined to live life to the fullest, and thought that a move to Oz could be the adventure she and her husband Adam needed. Adam was less than convinced, despite being the one to have the idea in the first place. Could a week of living the Aussie life make him see where Cheryl was coming from? Four years on, have they been able to come to a decision about where home should be? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife cider-makers Liz and Mike are excited to bring their cider, made from eight varieties of apples, to the South of England Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Andy, his wife Anna and their two young children Lillie and Daisy have spent the last few days gearing up for the show's poultry competition. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually, while Cherry Healey discovers the science behind smelly feet and tests brands of socks to see how they cope with the problem. Ruth Goodman looks back at how socks became a fashion statement in the 1980s and a vital piece of military kit in the First World War

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox tells the life story of the solar system, beginning with the development of the four rocky worlds closest to the Sun - Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. They were born at the same time from the same material - yet have lived radically different lives. What immense forces are at play?

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Thepchaiya Un-Nooh. Coverage of the first-round match on day two at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where Bingham gets the defence of his title under way. The former world champion faces an intriguing challenge against his Thai opponent, who is regarded as the quickest player on the tour and competing in the Masters for the first time in his career

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Interesting finds from the show's travels around the country, including a late-19th-century Steiff cow on rollers and a joystick from a World War One aeroplane

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick journeys inland from the majestic Camel Estuary to one of Britain's finest vineyards, where he joins in harvesting the grapes to discover that the Cornish weather is perfect making some of the best wine in the country. Just off Pentire Point, as the sun sets, Rick rides the gentle swell of the north Atlantic with an old friend, casting his line to hook Britain's fastest fish, before firing up the barbeque to cook his catch with a recipe inspired from a trip to Goa. Rick joins Tim Smit, best known for starting the hugely successful Eden Project, who reveals to Rick his new and ambitious idea in Cornwall

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces coverage of the FA Cup fourth-round draw, as the remaining sides start to look towards Wembley Stadium and their potential route to the latter stages

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contenders take to the black chair under the spotlight and the ticking clock to answer questions on their Specialist Subjects and general knowledge. John Humphrys asks the questions, which tonight are on The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin, Marlene Dumas, the life and poetry of Edward Thomas and Guy Burgess

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: A team of murder mystery enthusiasts take on three Beatles fans for a last chance to make the quarter-finals. They'll compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the quiz show

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the first of the quarter-finals, with two teams competing to reach the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: Documentary following the British family of actress Jiah Khan in a search for the truth about her death in Mumbai in 2013, which was ruled a suicide by police. Jiah's mother Rabbiya hired private forensic investigator Jason Payne James, who believes it is highly unlikely that a distinctive oval mark found on the 25-year-old's neck could be caused by the dupatta scarf she was found hanging from in her flat

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Doc Martin star Martin Clunes hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from October 2019, with Layla Moran and Sara Pascoe joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at that week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Shaun Murphy v Mark Williams. Action from the first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, as two former world champions looked to progress to the quarter-finals

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Thepchaiya Un-Nooh and Shaun Murphy v Mark Williams. The key moments from day two at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the first round continued

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce revisits some of the show's most exciting finds, and discovers what happened when the cameras stopped rolling. She reveals the outcome of the auction for a special guitar belonging to the Beatles and the mystery of the missing blueprint for the Royal Albert Hall, before going behind the scenes of Pinewood Film and TV Studios to learn about its wartime history, its famous costumes, and a giant golden gong

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: Spring brings an influx of wildlife to the islands, where the former Springwatch presenter is living for a year. Exploring the sea, Simon discovers starfish, moon jellyfish and gets close to a seal. He also follows the struggle at a wildlife sanctuary to teach an injured otter cub to use its back legs, and gives his daughter Savannah a lesson in birdwatching

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The former Monty Python star's expedition takes him to Senegal, where he witnesses a wrestling competition, visits jazz clubs, and meets soap star Marie-Madeleine. He also visits Bamako and Dogon country, then travels to Djenne, where he celebrates the Muslim Tabaski feast and secures a passage on a cargo boat to take him down the Niger River to Timbuktu

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou is offered a way out of jail if he agrees to become a police informer, but on the condition that he keeps away from Laure. Meanwhile, the police team focus their investigation on the Paris neighbourhood of Barbès, where vulnerable delinquent minors are being exploited by receivers of stolen goods. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: The police investigation screeches to a sudden halt when Souleymane disappears. How and where will they find him? Meanwhile, Laure flouts the ban on seeing Gilou, who has got himself hired as a bouncer in Cisco's nightclub. There he meets the security manager, Cisco's son Titi. In French

programme.title: Paris
programme.desc: Art historian Sandrine Voillet presents a guide to Paris, celebrating 800 years of its arts, design and architecture. She begins by visiting the Louvre, a fairy-tale park and the city's sewers, before heading to a restaurant known for its boudoir lounges. Sandrine also meets French photographer Willy Ronis and reveals the controversy surrounding the construction of the Eiffel Tower and the Basilica of Sacre Coeur

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on June 7, with performances by artists including Pop Will Eat Itself, Don Pablo's Animals, Elton John, D-Shake, the Mission, Wilson Phillips, MC Tunes versus 808 State, Was (Not Was) and Roxette

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on June 14, with performances by artists including Guru Josh, Maureen Walsh, Snap, Maxi Priest, Bobby Brown, the Charlatans, Betty Boo, New Kids on the Block, England and New Order and 49ers

programme.title: Island at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by artists associated with the Island record label during its 50 years. Tracks include Cat Stevens' Father and Son, Roxy Music's Do the Strand and Bob Marley and the Wailers' Stir It Up. The programme also features Steel Pulse, U2, PJ Harvey, Bob Marley, Amy Winehouse and Baaba Maal

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The former Monty Python star's expedition takes him to Senegal, where he witnesses a wrestling competition, visits jazz clubs, and meets soap star Marie-Madeleine. He also visits Bamako and Dogon country, then travels to Djenne, where he celebrates the Muslim Tabaski feast and secures a passage on a cargo boat to take him down the Niger River to Timbuktu

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: Spring brings an influx of wildlife to the islands, where the former Springwatch presenter is living for a year. Exploring the sea, Simon discovers starfish, moon jellyfish and gets close to a seal. He also follows the struggle at a wildlife sanctuary to teach an injured otter cub to use its back legs, and gives his daughter Savannah a lesson in birdwatching

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: Michael Mosley follows the progress of a group of people experiencing the tough living and working conditions of the Victorian poor after moving into a restored Victorian tenement in the heart of London's East End. In the opening edition the slum dwellers move into their new home, a building made up of sparse rooms, a shared water pump and outdoor privy toilets, while one finds out first-hand the back breaking labour his forebears would have experienced and the effect on a Victorian family when the main breadwinner was out of action

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary following solar scientist Dr Lucie Green as she reveals how some of the UK's worst and most dramatic weather disasters occurred many years ago, despite the popular belief that the brunt of climate change has been felt in the 21st century. Considering how many people today are pre-warned about potential catastrophes due to innovations in weather prediction, the presenter learns how an 18th-century storm surge once led to a the deaths of a thousand workers in Somerset fields, a hurricane drowned a fifth of Britain's naval officers, and several brutal winters threatened to completely shut down most services in the country

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Adaptation of John Le Carre's novel, starring Tom Hiddleston, Olivia Colman and Hugh Laurie. A former soldier working in a Cairo hotel discovers evidence against international arms dealer Richard Roper, but his attempt to help British intelligence to capture the criminal has disastrous consequences. Five years later, he is based in Switzerland, working at a hotel that he discovers is regularly frequented by Roper. Determined to make amends for his previous failure, he reunites with his contact at British intelligence and they form a plan to infiltrate the arms dealer's inner circle

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Pine and Burr hatch a plan to get Roper's attention by setting up a series of faked criminal activities, including stealing from a hotel and conducting drug deals in Devon. Their intention is for Pine to develop a reputation in the underworld, which could give him the chance to infiltrate the arms dealer's inner circle. Six months later, Roper's son is kidnapped by two thieves from his villa in Mallorca - but the abduction is not what it seems, nor is the mysterious figure lurking in the shadows

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Palace Secrets
programme.desc: The historian goes behind closed doors at three treasure-filled royal palaces to uncover how each building has shaped a monarchy and a nation. With exclusive access to the palaces' most secret places, she uncovers the dramatic events that changed the course of British history, from might and intimidation at the Tower of London and the rise and fall of absolute monarchy at Hampton Court, to the modern face of royalty at Kensington Palace

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: Michael Mosley follows the progress of a group of people experiencing the tough living and working conditions of the Victorian poor after moving into a restored Victorian tenement in the heart of London's East End. In the opening edition the slum dwellers move into their new home, a building made up of sparse rooms, a shared water pump and outdoor privy toilets, while one finds out first-hand the back breaking labour his forebears would have experienced and the effect on a Victorian family when the main breadwinner was out of action

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary following solar scientist Dr Lucie Green as she reveals how some of the UK's worst and most dramatic weather disasters occurred many years ago, despite the popular belief that the brunt of climate change has been felt in the 21st century. Considering how many people today are pre-warned about potential catastrophes due to innovations in weather prediction, the presenter learns how an 18th-century storm surge once led to a the deaths of a thousand workers in Somerset fields, a hurricane drowned a fifth of Britain's naval officers, and several brutal winters threatened to completely shut down most services in the country

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Shaun Murphy v Mark Williams. Coverage of the first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, as two former world champions look to progress to the quarter-finals

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic returns to examines more of the techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres, beginning by looking at the tropes of British comedies. Mark argues that they win laughs by tapping into the public's abiding national preoccupations, from rooting for the underdog to the subversion of class and manners, as well as the audience's fascination with comedy and crime, and pastiches and parodies that take the big beasts of Hollywood down a peg or two

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Apostol throws a lavish party to celebrate his daughter's birthday - but when the guest of honour is found dead, he becomes vulnerable and Burr is able to gain leverage over him. Roper digs deeper into Pine's fictitious criminal past and offers him a job in his organisation - but Corky remains suspicious of the newcomer. When Pine is given permission to take Daniel into town, he and Burr have a chance to share intelligence and deal with a potential threat to their plans by persuading the troubled Apostol to turn on Corky

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Pine is now firmly embedded in Roper's organisation and gets involved in an arms deal, but his relationship with Jed, and Corky's growing jealousy, jeopardise his cover. Burr puts pressure on Apostol to help her translate the information she has received on Roper's offshore operation, but begins to suspect the arms dealer has been buying weapons from the British government - a fear that Palfrey confirms. A tip-off leads her to Istanbul, but her attempt to catch Roper in the act puts Pine in further danger

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry is joined by actor Paul McGann and psychotherapist Philippa Perry to explore the technology, art and culture of the last years of Queen Victoria's reign. In the first episode, Hannah investigates how innovations in science and technology - including the X-ray, safety bicycle and proto-aeroplane - transformed Britain in the 1890s, with the end of the 19th century marking the beginning of a new 'Electric Age'

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Documentary about traditional Mexican crafts and those preserving them, beginning by following the making of a loose-fitting tunic known as a huipil

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic returns to examines more of the techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres, beginning by looking at the tropes of British comedies. Mark argues that they win laughs by tapping into the public's abiding national preoccupations, from rooting for the underdog to the subversion of class and manners, as well as the audience's fascination with comedy and crime, and pastiches and parodies that take the big beasts of Hollywood down a peg or two

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Four-part series giving an account of Barack Obama's successes and failures, as told by his inner circle and chief adversaries, including the story of how he tried to reshape America. The election of the first African American President marked an era of hope, but what happened instead was economic collapse, minimal cooperation from the Republican opposition, and the rise of a populist anti-big government Tea Party. The first edition focuses on how Obama crashed a private meeting of fellow heads of state to persuade the Chinese to tackle climate change at the Copenhagen summit, as well as his struggle to shut down Guantanamo

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: This Halloween is the spookiest ever for Arthur and friends. While trick-or-treating, Francine meets an old woman with a mysterious past, while Binky finds himself at Mr. Ratburn’s house. Muffy and Bailey decide to cut through a dark, rainy cemetery. And as for Arthur, Buster and Ladonna, their treehouse sleepover seems to be haunted... by a little sister and brother!

programme.desc: Dennis is desperate to prevent Pieface from failing Mrs Creecher's maths test, so persuades the gang to use Professor Von Screwtop's mind-swapping device. However, in a disastrous turn of events, Screwtop goes on the loose with the brain of Paul the potato and Pieface is left with a potato with the brain of Screwtop to help get him through the test

Cam draws the short straw and has to clean out the camel enclosure, while Billy the ocelot is given a pungent treat to play with and the boys recall the extraordinary story of how the zoo was started. Meanwhile, the brothers catch up on when they met with rangers on the front line training to intercept and arrest wildlife poachers.

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Join Lauren Layfield, the Dog and trainee teacher Mr Learner, for the brand new series of CBBC’s comedy panel show The Dog Ate My Homework. Today, Mr Learner is training to be a Design and Technology teacher and builds a very sophisticated A.I. teacher to help him out. What could possibly go wrong? Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Camilla and Noah, who are joined by special guests Richie Driss from Blue Peter and comedians Jarred Christmas, Marlon Davis and Jenny Bede. They all battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh. Rounds include Kids Have Arted and hysterical new game Boxymorons, where teams have to try and guess the object hidden inside a box. Plus the return of everyone’s favourite, Pie the Supply, before we finish up on School Trip, a new final round where two of the team players board the school bus to answer questions. Watch out for plenty of gunge and hilarity.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal.
