programme.desc: It's time for the annual Animal Obstacle Course Race - the animals need to work together in teams to complete the course. Duggee has his Obstacle Course badge, together with the squirrels they form the Red team and the fun begins

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are playing dress up when they hear a ghostly sound. They can see that something has spooked the chickens, so they stop what they are doing to investigate

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch worries his Halloween pumpkin has not grown big enough, and decides to raid the Gua Rusa cave in Borneo for some super-powered guano fertiliser

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The rabbit loses Cotton-tail's beloved squeaky toy, and must defeat his enemies to get it back

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: The duo help famous artist La Purl move a very large, heavy rock across the beach, as she needs it for a mysterious work of art. They try rolling it, but it's too heavy. Expert rock movers Bevel, Zip and Pop arrive, but can't move it either. Looking for things to help them, Bitz finds some logs to roll the rock, but Bevel stops to eat a banana and the rock rolls off out of control, causing the logs to go everywhere. They then consider floating the rock

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: A sea sponge that houses sea creatures in its pores suffers from a mysterious illness, and Peso must figure out the cause

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster arrives at the Lost Things Office ready to help Elder Bunny try and reunite the citizens of Fluffytown with possessions they might have lost.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Brown Cow takes to the stage with her friends Owl, Howl and Growl as the Alphablocks have fun with the sound 'ow'

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Things fail to go according to plan as Justin tries to claim his last - and most important - Rambling Ranger badge, the Wild Camping Badge

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Huffy's toot stick breaks, so Raa Raa tries to find the right sound to replace it

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ball and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: In their adventure, Dog and Pug meet Granny Pug who makes them solve three puzzles.

programme.title: Dipdap
programme.desc: The Line draws an egg for Dip Dap, who wants to stop it from breaking

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop finds Bing's carrot torch, and together they set off into the dark on an adventure

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos see a postman putting mail through a letterbox and decide to copy him

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a magic castle, and there's magic with The Great Tumbleeny!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends go 'this way, that way' on a Tubby Car journey to the Hidey Hup. The Tubby Spinny goes 'this way, that way' too

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: See how talking about things you do regularly helps your toddler feel safe and secure. Giovanna hosts from her own home. Join in with the Toddler Club Bop!

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The Potatoes go on an imaginary hippie journey and sing about the amazing things a person can do when they give their imagination a try

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It's winter and it's dark and too cold to play outside. The squirrels wonder what Duggee is up to - he seems to be looking through a long stick out of the window. Duggee explains that it's a telescope, and he's looking at things far away in space. The squirrels are keen to have a go too, but first Duggee tells them about the Solar System, and in order to help him they must all dress up as planets

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Close is practising her recorder for school assembly when a curious Topsy decides to join in with the music!

programme.title: Kiri & Lou
programme.desc: Lou eats too much sugar and runs amok at a poetry recital.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo feels 'bleugh', making it hard to choose between the library and the swimming pool

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a mosaic of a butterfly and a colour-changing robot, and watch as Book Girl makes a bridge

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie is visited by Grandad and Grandma on the farm. They help Ottalie pick apples from the tree, before Ottalie and Grandma get to work making a tasty apple sponge for pudding

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: The friends visit a museum and Woolly helps Tig learn the difference between imagination and reality

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Gurdy is having lots of little ideas, who run all over the tree. There's no room for the Olobobs! So they create Pie Piper, who enchants the ideas and gives them a yummy pie

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kay has invited his friends to taste his Polish dill pickle soup, which his mum has shown him how to cook all by himself

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's mum, Jess has just married Doug's Dad, Simon. They arrive home to find a puppy hiding in the wedding gifts. This magical, talking dog will change their lives for ever

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Otus gets a new remote-controlled truck, and Timmy resorts to a number of increasingly reckless tricks to play with it, until disaster strikes and the toy breaks

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: A fable from a collection of African folk tales about why a parrot cannot keep a secret

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It is O's Birthday but R has forgotten to get him a present, so tries to magic up a big wheel, helter skelter and hall of mirrors from thin air

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'mouse' and heads on a whirlwind word adventure. Dee meets lots of different mice - a brown mouse, a furry mouse and a whiskery mouse

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: When Nine needs a helping hand, he turns into a talented trio of circus blocks, the Three Threes. Plus, a look at how to share nine into equal parts

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny and Sula clamber around the trees in the park, but Bing climbs too high and discovers he needs some help

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy explores science questions that children ask about the world around us. Messy and his friends discover an amazing cave on their quest for the perfect sculpture material for OKIDO's most famous artist, Zinnie.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend play pizza restaurants, and Mr Tumble knits a jumper

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: During Tiddlytubbies' Sleepybyes, RuRu is wide awake. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po take it in turns to sing him a lullaby. When the Teletubbies sing the lullaby together, it soon sends RuRu off to sleep

programme.title: Mister Maker at Home
programme.desc: Mister Maker is having trouble using his trusty Makerphone and there’s only one friend who will be best placed to help him. He video-calls his friend Jen from CBeebies’s Andy’s Aquatic Adventures, which leads to arty inspiration! Mister Maker makes a pretend computer out of lots of card, stickers, fun foam and buttons. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister M makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art - he turns an old sock into something special! Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog Loves Bedtime Baboon? Dog and Pug go on an adventure to help Bedtime Baboon.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne meets Lulu and her pet dog Benji and together they head off to the vets to have Benji's dew claws removed. Meanwhile Rory travels to a safari park to join Paddy the vet who is examining one of the tigers

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ottalie goes on a trip to visit her Grandma, Grandpa and Heidi the pony

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace looks back at some of her favourite machines that are electric, fast and useful

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Harvest time arrives, and Duggee's garden is over-flowing with newly ripened crops, but there are so many it will take him forever to pick them all

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Tommy Brock decides he has had enough of the cold, so he takes shelter by the fire inside the Bouncer's burrow

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: The presenter travels back in time to the era of the Triceratops, a plant-eating dinosaur which roamed the plains of North America

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: On a camping trip to High Snowy Mountain, Bitz, Bob and Purl offer to help a very tired bear-sitter Bevel get two bear cubs to take their nap

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch goes on a desert adventure to uncover a lost city, but is disappointed to learn someone else has found it already

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly and her friends go litter-picking on the beach. Meanwhile, Moira has lost her precious whistle and no-one realises it's in Molly's bucket and about to get recycled

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle learns that reading can be just as much fun as noisy maze games

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran suggests they post JoJo's picture to Great Gran Gran in Saint Lucia. JoJo learns why a letter takes such a long time to travel halfway across the world.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Rainbow's colourful glow inspires an art project

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: When Pepi Nana loses her red pencil, Little Nana finds it under the wardrobe. Because she is small, Little Nana sees things that Pepi Nana might not see. The two friends go for a walk, and along the way Little Nana shows Pepi Nana lots of new things

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small Clanger finds a strange ball while out fishing in space. No one knows what it is so Major decides to put it on a shelf in his workshop. But a little while later he finds it in two hollow pieces on the ground - and the Soup Dragon discovers that something has been eating the copper trees. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: A ball bounces around the garden and visits the characters one by one, until it is blown away by Makka Pakka's Uff-uff

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Laura Haddock reads Sam Hay's Star in a Jar, about a little boy who finds a very special treasure. However, when it becomes unhappy, he realises he cannot keep it. With his big sister's help, he tries to help his new friend make its way home

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Anna Haugh, and guest Tamzin Outhwaite, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to what is one of the most eagerly anticipated weekends of the football calendar, when the FA Cup third round takes place

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. Experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young offer support, while away from the action Charlie sees a selection of of rare toys

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, with a £400,000 budget to find their ideal home near the English Riviera. Away from the property hunt, Ginny capitalises on being in Devon at the same time as the County Show to find out what it takes to win Pig of the Year

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the FA Cup, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Newcastle United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, which takes place at Emirates Stadium, as the holders take on Steve Bruce's Magpies. The Gunners have struggled badly in the Premier League so far this season, and may see this competition as a welcome distraction. The sides last met in this tournament at the fourth-round stage in 2008, when an Emmanuel Adebayor double and a Nicky Butt own goal sealed a routine 3-0 victory for Arsene Wenger's team

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob wants more from Connie, but she is unsure if will she be able to make the time for their relationship with mounting pressures on the emergency department. Meanwhile, Faith confronts Lev about his secret, and new paramedic Leon arrives to meet the rest of the team and complete a Covid-19 training course

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has never been very good at DIY, finding even the simple task of hanging a picture invariably ending in disaster. So it remains to be seen how he will cope after turning up at one of Wales's biggest building sites and being expected to get stuck in. From bricklaying to plumbing and carpentry, Rhod has to go all out to prove he has what it takes to become a master builder

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of the Wild Card play-off games, as the post-season begins

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned, then follow the sale and talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings. After three months the team returns to review their progress

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian advise two teams scouring London's vibrant Portobello Road for items to take to auction. Presented by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is in Stirling exploring the city's connection to the world-famous King James Bible, and JB Gill meets a Scottish singer who found Jesus while battling addiction

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Crawley Town v Leeds United (Kick-off 1.30pm). Coverage of the third-round tie at the People's Pension Stadium, where the League Two hosts are aiming to cause an upset. Crawley's league form this season has been inconsistent, leaving them firmly in mid-table, but they've already been involved in one of the most remarkable matches in this season's FA Cup, winning 6-5 away to Torquay United after extra time. They shocked AFC Wimbledon in round two, and are aiming to make a name for themselves against the Premier League visitors

programme.title: Carol Vorderman: Closer to Home
programme.desc: Carol takes a road trip across her home patch of North Wales and reflects on how the region has shaped her. The journey begins on the coast in her hometown of Prestatyn, sharing memories of her mother's life as a divorcee raising three children alone in the 1960s and reflecting on how the lives of women have changed since then

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Marine v Tottenham Hotspur (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the third-round encounter at Rossett Park, where the non-league side welcome Jose Mourinho's Premier League high-flyers. If ever a match epitomised the magic of the cup, then this is surely it, with Marine being only the second side from the eighth tier of English football to reach the third round. They have already won seven fixtures to reach this stage, including a penalty shootout triumph over League Two Colchester United in round one. Spurs are expected to rest several of their key players following a hectic festive period, but will be wary of being on the wrong end of one of the greatest upsets in the competition's history should they take Marine too lightly

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: After the turmoil of 2020 and as a new year gets underway, the team looks at how people can plan to make the countryside a bigger part of their lives? Matt Baker wants to get fitter and tries his hand at cani-cross, Anita Rani straps on her walking boots, Margherita Taylor heads for the woods and Helen Skelton aims to buy local. Tom Heap discovers the joys of 'blue' therapy and Adam Henson gets creative with clay

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth - the Sun - and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles befriends homesick young French backpacker Dominique Renelleau, introducing himself as Alain Gautier. After dinner, Dominique falls sick so Charles takes him back to his Bangkok apartment, where he promises that he and Marie-Andrée will nurse him back to health. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Action from the latest third-round fixtures, with Marine v Tottenham Hotspur, Newport County v Brighton & Hove Albion and Chelsea v Morecambe among the highlights. All three of the Premier League sides would have expected their class to tell against lower-league opposition, but the magic of the cup always suggests that a shock isn't too far around the corner. Plus, action from Barnsley v Tranmere Rovers, Bristol City v Portsmouth, Cheltenham Town v Mansfield Town, Crawley Town v Leeds United and Manchester City v Birmingham City

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: Flying Blind (2012)
programme.desc: An aerospace engineer designing military drones begins a passionate affair with a younger Muslim student. The relationship leads to her being questioned by the security services, who claim he is involved in terrorism, forcing her to question how much she knows about her lover and where her loyalties lie. Drama, starring Helen McCrory and Najib Oudghiri

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the scammers selling non-existent motorhomes to unsuspecting customers, and the cyber criminals who hijack bookings for holiday rentals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down bungalow in Earley in Berkshire, a Victorian terrace with lots of period features in Norwich and a pair of semis in Burton-on-Trent with a fabulous river view are all sold under the hammer. All the developers have ambitious plans for their auction buys and hope to make some money, so Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell monitor whether their ideas are realised

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After a terrible accident turned their lives upside down, Stan and Andrea thought Australia was the answer. Six years on, did the dream become reality? Nikki Chapman finds out

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Dave Myers enjoys a taste of his native county when he and Si King demonstrate recipes using produce from Cumbria, including a supper dish made with Cumberland sausage

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £450,000, a married couple looking to uproot from Greater Manchester seek the help of Jules Hudson to find a new family home close to the coast in Dorset

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families prepare for the farmers' market in historic Stokesley - rare-breed pig farmers Nigel and Benjamin, and the Mellanby family with their harvested vegetables

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy items, including two teddy bears, an Islamic tile, a painting, and a chair brought in by a 12-year-old boy. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley is stunned when she learns the truth, prompting her to take matters into her own hands. Denise is left questioning who she should believe

programme.title: Nightshifters
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the hidden world of night-time workers - those unsung heroes who occupy the graveyard shift in a wide range of jobs. The first programme features whelk fishermen leaving Saundersfoot harbour, train cleaners in Cardiff and a lone Swansea taxi driver employed over the witching hour. This invisible army works in the dark to make sure the day runs like clockwork

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: McKinven's initial enquiries ruffle feathers, while a furious Julie tears into Emma for dragging up the past. When Skye threatens Emma with a knife, it is clear that someone is hiding something. Molly Windsor stars.

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: The actors spot an opportunity to sabotage the show and while David is deflated to discover how people feel about him, Michael is overjoyed to discover how they feel about him. Anna and Georgia consider their options Comedy in which David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Struggling to get any more information from Ashley, Rex turns to a forensic psychologist for answers. A date for trial is set, but Ashley makes a shocking announcement

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: A guide to strengthening the immune system during cold and flu season, examining some of the so-called 'miracle' products, superfoods and supplements that claim to help

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Festive instalment of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to visit some of the many islands that lay off the coastline

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian advise two teams scouring London's vibrant Portobello Road for items to take to auction. Presented by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is in Stirling exploring the city's connection to the world-famous King James Bible, and JB Gill meets a Scottish singer who found Jesus while battling addiction

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Crawley Town v Leeds United (Kick-off 1.30pm). Coverage of the third-round tie at the People's Pension Stadium, where the League Two hosts are aiming to cause an upset. Crawley's league form this season has been inconsistent, leaving them firmly in mid-table, but they've already been involved in one of the most remarkable matches in this season's FA Cup, winning 6-5 away to Torquay United after extra time. They shocked AFC Wimbledon in round two, and are aiming to make a name for themselves against the Premier League visitors

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: In the Hebrides and Arran, Michael Angus, Anna Campbell-Jones and Kate Spiers visit an art-filled family home, a contemporary hillside build, and a New York loft-style abode

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Marine v Tottenham Hotspur (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the third-round encounter at Rossett Park, where the non-league side welcome Jose Mourinho's Premier League high-flyers. If ever a match epitomised the magic of the cup, then this is surely it, with Marine being only the second side from the eighth tier of English football to reach the third round. They have already won seven fixtures to reach this stage, including a penalty shootout triumph over League Two Colchester United in round one. Spurs are expected to rest several of their key players following a hectic festive period, but will be wary of being on the wrong end of one of the greatest upsets in the competition's history should they take Marine too lightly

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: After the turmoil of 2020 and as a new year gets underway, the team looks at how people can plan to make the countryside a bigger part of their lives? Matt Baker wants to get fitter and tries his hand at cani-cross, Anita Rani straps on her walking boots, Margherita Taylor heads for the woods and Helen Skelton aims to buy local. Tom Heap discovers the joys of 'blue' therapy and Adam Henson gets creative with clay

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth - the Sun - and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles befriends homesick young French backpacker Dominique Renelleau, introducing himself as Alain Gautier. After dinner, Dominique falls sick so Charles takes him back to his Bangkok apartment, where he promises that he and Marie-Andrée will nurse him back to health. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Action from the latest third-round fixtures, with Marine v Tottenham Hotspur, Newport County v Brighton & Hove Albion and Chelsea v Morecambe among the highlights. All three of the Premier League sides would have expected their class to tell against lower-league opposition, but the magic of the cup always suggests that a shock isn't too far around the corner. Plus, action from Barnsley v Tranmere Rovers, Bristol City v Portsmouth, Cheltenham Town v Mansfield Town, Crawley Town v Leeds United and Manchester City v Birmingham City

programme.title: Flying Blind (2012)
programme.desc: An aerospace engineer designing military drones begins a passionate affair with a younger Muslim student. Drama, starring Helen McCrory and Najib Oudghiri

programme.title: Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal
programme.desc: Tom Brook marks the fortieth anniversary of former Beatle John Lennon's murder in New York on 8 December 1980. His untimely death was a slaying that shocked millions of fans around the world. In Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal, the BBC's Tom Brook, the first British journalist to report live from the site of the New York shooting, takes stock of Lennon's life and the legacy of his music. Brook recounts what happened on the night Lennon died when he returned to his home, the Dakota apartment building on Manhattan's Upper West Side, with his wife Yoko Ono, and he hears from some of Lennon's closest confidantes as well as younger Lennon fans in America today. Drawing on a wealth of archive material, the programme includes Tom Brook's interview with the former Beatle's widow Yoko Ono, one of the first she gave, in which she discussed Lennon's life and death. The programme assesses John Lennon's powerful legacy 40 years on and asks whether his messages of love and peace are still re

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: The course of the seasons, beginning with spring, which sees the hills on fire on fire as the annual muirburn takes place in the Cairngorms. As the flames rise, an osprey returns to a valley near Loch Ness, with its movement captured in super slow-motion at 800 frames per second. Red squirrels and roe deer prepare for the breeding season, while a cataclysmic downpour has major consequences for a family of dippers, whose youngsters are forced to fledge early before the nest is swept away. Narrated by Ewan McGregor

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)

programme.title: Na Tiristich (From Tiree with Love)

programme.title: Niteworks: Baile air Bhoil

programme.title: Sulaisgeir
programme.desc: For centuries, men from Ness in the Isle of Lewis in the Western Isles have sailed to a tiny island, 40 miles North of Lewis each summer to collect their harvest of seabirds. Dods MacFarlane has followed in the footsteps of his forefathers for over 40 years, but here he leads the annual guga hunt for the last time

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.desc: Feeling a little bored, Po and the kids are excited to accept an important invitation to the Forbidden City.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guest Jenni Falconer and chefs Atul Kochhar and Peter Lloyd, who serve up prawn and monkfish curry, and Indonesian fish

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode shares a masterclass in making the perfect tuna steak served with a gremolata, while Lisa Faulkner is serving up comfort food with her oven-roasted chicken noodle soup

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The presenter visits North Wales, and is joined by fellow culinary expert Stephen Terry. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad with local chef Debra Sardinha as his guide, visiting a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea. Ainsley explores Port of Spain's market in search of ingredients to make grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter explores the country's most famous and best-loved landscapes and experiences, beginning by going on safari at the Amakhala Game Reserve in the Eastern Cape. After a drive through the reserve, he camps out overnight and tastes his first poike - a traditional South African stew - cooked over a campfire. In the morning, Gregg joins vet Will Fowlds and his team as they care for a wild leopard

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps, cute kids and practical jokes, including a car bonnet flying open on a motorway, a teenager terrified by a seagull and a toddler catching out her family with a classic gag - fake poo

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants David, Maureen, Luke and Alison pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Richard Ayoade, Katie McGlynn and Joe Swash all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Disco queen Sophie Ellis-Bextor and Spice Girl Mel B were the first two secret singers revealed in weeks one and two respectively. Now, another famous face is unmasked and eliminated - but not before Swan, Badger, Robin, Dragon and Sausage have taken to the stage to perform. Presented by Joel Dommett, with panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the spinning-chair singing contest, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The four coaches will once again be selecting their teams through blind auditions, where looks play no part in the selection process and each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts a TV-themed comedy quiz show, joined by team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett. Taking part in this first edition are Denise van Outen, Adil Ray, Catherine Tyldesley and Josh Widdicombe, who all compete to test their TV knowledge

programme.title: Rush Hour 2 (2001)
programme.desc: The two mismatched detectives travel to Hong Kong when terrorists bomb the US embassy, and are soon involved in keeping tabs on the prime suspects - high-ranking Triad members involved in an international counterfeiting ring. Action comedy sequel, starring Jackie Chan, Chris Tucker, John Lone, Zhang Ziyi, Roselyn Sanchez and Harris Yulin

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Vick, Sam and Frankie are feeling competitive as they find out how to race bugs, Sam gets gross when he asks how gruesome your face is, and food gets dangerous with Doctor HOW's digestive biscuits and Frankie's carrot.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter walks through the two counties, which boast more than 5,000 miles of footpaths, from the rugged rocks of Dartmoor to the picturesque port of Padstow. In this first episode, Julia takes on a stunning 6.5-mile route on Cornwall's most westerly point. She experiences the iconic landmark of Land's End, visits the famous white sands of Porthcurno beach and the historic clifftop Minack Theatre, before finishing her journey at Sennen Cove

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh on the farm this week are Kevin Clifton, Michael Portillo and Matthew Kelly. Plus, a look at the best British pork pies and pints

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode shares a masterclass in making the perfect tuna steak served with a gremolata, while Lisa Faulkner is serving up comfort food with her oven-roasted chicken noodle soup

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania (2012)
programme.desc: Dracula opens a luxury resort for monsters, to provide a refuge from terrified humans. His cosy sanctuary is disrupted when a normal man unwittingly checks in, and the situation is complicated when the unwanted guest takes a liking to the count's daughter. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as Rezwan and Zach, Paul and Charlotte, and Anna and Laura use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leave with nothing

programme.title: Bean (1997)
programme.desc: The hapless fool is employed as a security guard for a British art gallery and is sent to Los Angeles to bring back a famous painting. Unfortunately, he finds himself mistaken for a renowned art professor and invited to make an important presentation. Mel Smith's British comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Burt Reynolds, Peter MacNicol, John Mills, Peter Capaldi and June Brown

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the game show in which two families guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to play for the £30,000 jackpot. This time, it's the turn of the Craig family from Falkirk, who take on the Mills family from Essex

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless fool sits an exam, but when he realises he has studied the wrong maths equations, it is already too late. He also tries to stifle a sneeze in church. Rowan Atkinson stars in the first episode of the celebrated comedy, with Richard Briers, Rudolph Walker and Howard Goodall

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: A routine trip to the dentist ends disastrously for Bean, and a quiet stroll in the park gradually turns into a pitched battle with a wasp. Rowan Atkinson stars, with appearances by Richard Wilson and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Happy Birthday Mr Bean
programme.desc: How the sitcom character - who featured in just 14 original episodes - has gone on to become a global superstar with an animated series and two top-grossing feature films. Rowan Atkinson's alter-ego has become an online phenomenon, with more than 110 million online fans and growing. This documentary features classic clips and interviews with Richard Curtis, Tony Robinson and Angus Deayton

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Paul 'The Sinnerman' Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: A look back at the infamous game of Charles Ingram, the man the press later dubbed, the Coughing Major. Jeremy Clarkson and Chris Tarrant are joined by former celebrity contestants and other big winners to recount their memories of Charles' unforgettable journey to the million pound question. Plus, Laurence Llwellyn-Bowen recalls getting a second chance to win a million

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son hit the road together again, but this time they are staying closer to home and heading for Europe as they travel from Essex to Italy's Amalfi coast. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken implores Peter to consider Daniel's offer and not to throw his life away, and Simon urges Leanne to see that Nick is suffering too. Faye confides in Maria that living with the guilt is making her ill and she'd rather own up to her crime

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders
programme.desc: Drama based on the true story of John Cooper, a serial killer who evaded justice for more than 20 years. Two unsolved double murders from the 1980s cast a shadow over the work of the Dyfed Powys police force, so in 2006, newly promoted Detective Superintendent Steve Wilkins decides to reopen both cases. Starring Luke Evans, Keith Allen and Alexandria Riley

programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of social worker Jenny Lister, but figuring out the motive behind the killing of an apparently popular woman proves difficult. However, an infamous past case involving the death of a child appears to hold the key. In her pursuit of the truth, Vera uncovers the failings of an understaffed care system and discovers the secret at the heart of Jenny's death - which the killer will stop at nothing to protect. Drama based on the book by Ann Cleeves, starring Brenda Blethyn and David Leon

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: The Cruise

Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old Susan Webb is desperate find the daughter she gave up for adoption in 1968. At the time, she was suffering with depression, and after being treated with electroconvulsive therapy that severely affected her memory, she was left with no recollections of her child. Meanwhile, Sonia Walsh looks for the mother that abandoned her at the age of three with her younger sister. During her investigations, she discovered her mother had also given up another baby, and she is now determined to make up for the pain of the past. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. The council gives a ruling on Ray's development, Grace turns up on Michael's doorstep, and Evelyn regrets her harsh words to Arthur

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of social worker Jenny Lister, but figuring out the motive behind the killing of an apparently popular woman proves difficult. However, an infamous past case involving the death of a child appears to hold the key. In her pursuit of the truth, Vera uncovers the failings of an understaffed care system and discovers the secret at the heart of Jenny's death - which the killer will stop at nothing to protect. Drama based on the book by Ann Cleeves, starring Brenda Blethyn and David Leon

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Romance is in the air for casino host Cecilia and assistant maitre d' Alberto - and she already has clear ideas about the size of her engagement ring. Things are not so happy for waitress Valeria, whose relationship ended when her boyfriend left the ship and now a close friend is leaving too. However, it isn't long until a new beau catches her eye. Timothy came on board after splitting from his partner of four years - but he has little time to worry about his broken heart in his new job with the shore excursions team, especially when he runs into hot water with a couple of misplaced passengers

programme.desc: Mildred becomes broody and starts to dream of motherhood, much to George's dismay - and they consider adoption. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When Mildred finds out that George only has a small endowment, she decides to do something about it. However, George is none too happy when he realises that he is worth more dead than alive. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective's former colleague, Sergeant Stuart Macken, is left badly burned when his house is petrol-bombed, while his teenage daughter fights for her life in intensive care. As Vera tries to discover why anyone would attempt to kill Stuart, her investigation leads to unpleasant revelations about his personal life, and she has to face long-buried memories of her own. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Steven Hartley and Julie Graham

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A feature-length case for Sir Arthur Conan Doyle's celebrated cerebral sleuth. The vicar of the village of Lamberley asks Holmes to look into several mysterious deaths that have taken place since the arrival of a man said to be descended from vampires. Though the village is gripped by fear and illness, Holmes remains convinced that there is a rational explanation.

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are at Kempton Park ahead of another competitive day of racing, with today's feature race being the 2021 renewal of the Lanzarote Hurdle

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Boxer Mike Tyson and darts player Eric Bristow discussing their crafts, there os a profile of Red Rum, and Martin O'Neill and Roy Keane talk about their managerial relationship

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Bogus Journey (1991)
programme.desc: The would-be heavy metal superstars face the Grim Reaper as they try to reclaim their souls from Hell, return to the land of the living and stop their robot doppelgangers from changing history. Along the way, they must also master their instruments, so they can triumph at the Battle of the Bands. Comedy sequel, starring Keanu Reeves, Alex Winter, William Sadler, Joss Ackland and George Carlin

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage from Chepstow, Kempton Park and Wincanton, including the rescheduled Welsh Grand National at Chepstow. Kempton stages four of the races, including the Lanzarote Handicap Hurdle and the Relkeel Hurdle, while the Dipper Novices' Chase is the feature race of the day at Wincanton. Analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels (1988)
programme.desc: A professional conman makes a career of seducing and swindling wealthy women on the French Riviera. However, his idyllic lifestyle is threatened by the arrival of a brash American trickster eager for a piece of the action. The pair make a wager - if one manages to trick a glamorous heiress out of her fortune, the other will agree to leave town. Comedy, with Michael Caine, Steve Martin, Glenne Headly and Ian McDiarmid

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is repaid a debt in dodgy £20 notes, but by the time it comes to light they are counterfeit his creditor is heading on a flight to India. Once the Winchester is pinpointed as the source of the forged currency, Arthur also finds he has Chisholm breathing down his neck. Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: There appears to be evidence that some of the world's most iconic rivers are in declining health, prompting biologist, extreme angler and naturalist Jeremy Wade to investigate the disappearance of freshwater giants from the planet's largest waterways. Here, he heads to the River Danube in search of two giant beasts - a fish so valuable criminals are hunting it out of existence and a man-sized predator that has become the stuff of dreams. What does the fate of these creatures say about the health of Europe's second longest river?

programme.title: The Avengers

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: An ex-footballer persuades Arthur to buy 2,000 tickets for an England v Scotland football match. However, trouble ensues once it becomes apparent they are forgeries. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle teams up with a Hong Kong cop, who is in London trailing a Triad gang supplying drugs to US army personnel in Germany while operating an international protection racket. programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess is accused of shooting dead her millionaire employer's wife on a remote bridge - but Holmes' deductive powers suggest an alternative explanation to the mystery. Period detective drama, guest starring Daniel Massey, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The unlikely duo investigate a case of caviar smuggling that led to murder. Feature-length episode of the detective thriller, starring Glynis Barber and Michael Brandon

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Ms Peel investigate the case of several businessman who have been killed by birds of prey - and discover a connection to a comic-book character

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A wealthy family insists Dr Sudbury, a hypnotist friend of Arthur's, has mesmerised its teenage daughter Rita into agreeing to marry him. Arthur finds himself immersed in the fracas, while simultaneously trying to take over the affairs of a steam-cleaning business. Light-hearted drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Emmerdale's Natalie Anderson (Alicia Metcalfe), actress Patsy Kensit, broadcaster Esther Rantzen and singer-presenter Brian McFadden compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage from Chepstow, Kempton Park and Wincanton, including the rescheduled Welsh Grand National at Chepstow. Kempton stages four of the races, including the Lanzarote Handicap Hurdle and the Relkeel Hurdle, while the Dipper Novices' Chase is the feature race of the day at Wincanton. Analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels (1988)
programme.desc: A professional conman makes a career of seducing and swindling wealthy women on the French Riviera. However, his idyllic lifestyle is threatened by the arrival of a brash American trickster eager for a piece of the action. The pair make a wager - if one manages to trick a glamorous heiress out of her fortune, the other will agree to leave town. Comedy, with Michael Caine, Steve Martin, Glenne Headly and Ian McDiarmid

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's obsession with gift-giving etiquette annoys his friends as Christmas approaches. With guest star Michael Trucco (Battlestar Galactica, One Tree Hill)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef again comes to the aid of failing American restaurants, starting in Fishtown, Philadelphia, where he tries to help the three owners of the Hot Potato Cafe, which has not made a profit in two years. Spurred on by a scathing review by an influential critic, he makes radical changes to alter the staff's attitudes and create food that is up to his own exacting standards

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer talk to Vick Hope, Vernon Kay, Don Warrington, Dane Baptiste, Kirstie Allsopp, Phil Spencer, Rick Stein and Eddie Hearn. Plus, Kara Marni performs in the studio

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A tax audit leads Homer into big trouble, leaving him with no choice but to make amends by helping the government nail Mr Burns for the theft of a trillion-dollar banknote

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer rent an apartment in an affluent area so their children can have a good education when Springfield Elementary is rated the worst school in the state. Bart wastes no time in establishing a bad-boy persona, and increases Lisa's popularity by telling people she knows a pop star. With the voice of Elliot Page (Juno)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge takes Lisa for her first manicure at the salon, where they debate whether a woman can be smart, beautiful and powerful all at the same time. Four tales featuring strong women from history and literature are retold with Selma appearing as Elizabeth I, Lisa as Snow White, Marge as Lady Macbeth and Maggie as Howard Roark from The Fountainhead

programme.title: A Dog's Purpose (2017)
programme.desc: A dog is reincarnated as different breeds over several generations, with multiple owners and with a variety of names, but he is fated to be reacquainted, decades later, with his original owner. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Quaid, with the voice of Josh Gad

programme.title: The Spiderwick Chronicles (2008)
programme.desc: Life for identical twins Jared and Simon and their sister Mallory begins afresh when their mother inherits a spooky mansion. programme.title: Channel 4 News

Other destinations include Edinburgh, where Julie discovers the intriguing connections between the railway and the famous Balmoral Hotel's Palm Court and Clock Tower

programme.desc: Catherine takes her first steps toward a coup, attempting to seduce Orlo in hopes of recruiting him to her cause. programme.desc: Intercut with dramatised extracts, this documentary relives the meteoric highs and devastating lows of the singer and actress's career in showbusiness. It lifts the lid on the crucial events and key players in her life - from abandonment by a drug-addict father and her time in an orphanage as a baby, to her mother's frustrated acting career with a revolving door of husbands. Cher's relationship with Sonny Bono is also explored - a longed-for father figure whose controlling ways almost broke her

programme.title: The World's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Everything from its enormous size to its art deco styling and its unmistakable colour make San Francisco's Golden Gate Bridge an icon of world design. Rob Bell explores the fascinating history of an edifice that people claimed was impossible to build, and those who created it anyway. Joseph Strauss was the arrogant showman promoting the project, while Charles Ellis and Leon Moisseiff were the mathematical geniuses behind the Golden Gate's cutting-edge design. Since its completion in 1937, it has received a staggering 10 million tourists every year
